seoTitle: How to Show Subtasks in a Jira Board
metaDescription: Get subtasks to appear as their own cards on a Jira board so you can track granular work alongside parent stories and tasks.
intro: Subtasks in Jira are sometimes hidden inside their parent issue, which makes it hard to track individual progress on a board. This guide explains how to expose subtasks as visible cards so your team can move them independently.

steps.heading: Open the board settings
steps.body: From your board, go into the settings menu where card layout and issue type visibility are controlled.

steps.heading: Find the subtask display option
steps.body: Look for a setting that controls whether subtasks appear as separate cards or stay nested inside their parent.

steps.heading: Enable subtasks as cards
steps.body: Turn on the option to show subtasks individually, so each one gets its own position on the board.

steps.heading: Adjust your board filter if needed
steps.body: If subtasks still aren't visible, check your board's saved filter to make sure subtask issue types aren't being excluded.

steps.heading: Confirm on the board
steps.body: Refresh the board and verify subtasks now move through columns independently from their parent story.

faq.question: Why are my subtasks not showing up on the board?
faq.answer: Most commonly the board's filter or card layout settings are excluding subtasks, so they stay hidden inside the parent issue.

faq.question: Can subtasks have a different status than their parent?
faq.answer: Yes, once visible as cards, subtasks move through the workflow independently of the parent issue's status.

faq.question: Does showing subtasks work on both Scrum and Kanban boards?
faq.answer: Both board types support displaying subtasks as cards, though the setting is configured separately for each.

faq.question: Will this clutter my board with too many cards?
faq.answer: It can on large boards, so many teams only enable subtask cards temporarily or use swimlanes to keep things organized.
